21.correct: The Supreme Court of India is the guardian (protector) of the Constitution, through its power of judicial review.

wrong.A: The President is the constitutional head but not the guardian in this sense.
wrong.C: The Prime Minister heads the executive.
wrong.D: Parliament makes laws but the Supreme Court guards the Constitution.

21.fact: The Supreme Court can strike down laws that violate the Constitution.

22.correct: The Chief Election Commissioner holds office for 6 years or until the age of 65, whichever is earlier.
22.fact: The CEC can be removed only in the same manner as a Supreme Court judge.

23.correct: Weight is a force, so its SI unit is the newton (N).

wrong.A: The kilogram is the SI unit of mass, not weight.
wrong.C: The joule is the unit of energy.
wrong.D: The pascal is the unit of pressure.

23.fact: Weight = mass x acceleration due to gravity (W = mg).

24.correct: Ruskin Bond's first novel was 'The Room on the Roof', for which he won the John Llewellyn Rhys Prize in 1957.

wrong.A: 'The Blue Umbrella' is a later novella.
wrong.B: 'A Flight of Pigeons' is a later work.
wrong.D: 'Our Trees Still Grow in Dehra' won the Sahitya Akademi Award (1992).

24.fact: Ruskin Bond wrote 'The Room on the Roof' as a teenager.

25.correct: The historic 1983 Cricket World Cup final, where India beat the West Indies, was played at Lord's, London.

wrong.B: It was not at The Oval.
wrong.D: It was not at Old Trafford.

25.fact: Kapil Dev's India won the 1983 World Cup, a turning point for Indian cricket.

26.correct: Import Substitution Industrialisation (ISI) aimed to replace imported manufactured goods with domestic production through protectionist policies.

wrong.A: It was not about promoting exports (that is export-led growth).
wrong.B: It was not about attracting foreign investment.
wrong.D: It was not about liberalizing imports.

26.fact: ISI was the dominant strategy in India from the 1950s until the 1991 reforms.

27.correct: Virender Sehwag was the first Indian batsman to score a triple century in Test cricket (309 vs Pakistan, 2004).

wrong.A: Sachin Tendulkar did not score a Test triple century.
wrong.C: Rahul Dravid did not score a triple century.
wrong.D: Karun Nair later scored a triple century, but after Sehwag.

27.fact: Sehwag scored two Test triple centuries, the only Indian to do so.

28.correct: FDI (Foreign Direct Investment) limits in India were liberalised in 1991 as part of the economic reforms.

wrong.B: 1995 is not when the major liberalization began.

28.fact: The 1991 reforms opened many sectors to foreign investment.

29.correct: The Credit Guarantee Fund for MSMEs and startups aims to provide collateral-free guarantee support for loans.

wrong.B: It is not a research grant.
wrong.C: It is not a pension fund.

29.fact: The CGTMSE scheme enables banks to lend to small enterprises without collateral.

30.correct: The Ganga-Bhagirathi-Hooghly system is part of India's National Waterways network (National Waterway 1).

wrong.B: The Indus is not a designated Indian national waterway.
wrong.C: The Mahanadi is not the one referred to here.
wrong.D: The Godavari has waterway stretches but the classic answer is the Ganga-Bhagirathi-Hooghly.

30.fact: National Waterway 1 runs from Haldia to Prayagraj on the Ganga system.

31.correct: Both given pairs are wrong - anaerobic respiration occurs in the ABSENCE of oxygen and aerobic in the PRESENCE of oxygen, the reverse of what is stated. So 'Neither I nor II' is correct.

wrong.A: 'Only I' is wrong; the pairing is reversed.
wrong.B: 'Only II' is wrong; the pairing is reversed.
wrong.C: 'Both' is wrong since both pairings are incorrect.

31.fact: Aerobic respiration needs oxygen; anaerobic respiration does not.

32.correct: The Swabhav Swachhata Sanskar Swachhata (4S) campaign 2024 was built around 3 key pillars.

wrong.B: 5 is not the number of pillars.

32.fact: The campaign was part of the Swachhata Hi Seva drive.

33.correct: Samudragupta became the Gupta ruler after Chandragupta I.

wrong.D: Chandragupta II succeeded Samudragupta, not the other way round.

33.fact: Samudragupta is called the 'Napoleon of India' for his many military conquests.

34.correct: As of March 2025, about 802 One-Stop Centres (OSCs) were operational across India to help women in distress.
34.fact: One-Stop Centres ('Sakhi' centres) provide integrated support to women affected by violence.

35.correct: The 'Deodhani' dance is associated with Assam.

wrong.B: It is not from Tamil Nadu.
wrong.C: It is not from Himachal Pradesh.

35.fact: The Deodhani dance of Assam is linked to the worship of the goddess Manasa.

36.correct: The GSLV Mk-III (LVM3) can carry up to about 4,000 kg to Geostationary Transfer Orbit (GTO).

wrong.B: 8500 kg is closer to its LEO capacity, not GTO.

36.fact: LVM3 can carry about 8,000 kg to Low Earth Orbit and ~4,000 kg to GTO.

37.correct: The Government of India Act, 1935 provided for provincial autonomy, ending dyarchy in the provinces.

wrong.B: The 1919 Act introduced dyarchy in the provinces.

37.fact: Under the 1935 Act, provincial elections were held in 1937.

38.correct: Between NFHS-4 and NFHS-5, the use of clean cooking fuel rose from 44% to about 59%.
38.fact: The PM Ujjwala Yojana helped increase access to clean LPG cooking fuel.

39.correct: In tennis, after deuce a player/team must win 2 consecutive points (advantage, then game) to win the game.

wrong.A: Winning only 1 point gives 'advantage', not the game.

39.fact: If the player who has advantage loses the next point, the score returns to deuce.

40.correct: The North-Eastern Region District SDG Index 2023-24 covered 121 districts of the north-east.
40.fact: The NER District SDG Index is prepared by NITI Aayog to track district-level SDG progress.
